Hey guys, JB here, talking to you daily motivation slash skill string. Day number three, week three state management for those who want certification, for those who aren't. I don't know, you should get on it. Anyway, here's the deal, right? You're probably much either through the core learning lessons here of this module and you're at the point where I've showed you step by step how to set an anchor and I'm not sure you've seen the live clip where I do it, but if you haven't seen that, go through that as well, the bonus lesson where I show you how to actually step by step do it and do it in front of a live audience. One of the things I want to just tell you here guys is that in practical terms, right, you know, we go through these sort of mini dips and rises, ebbs and flows in our state day in and day out, even intro day. So just don't take it for granted that if you show up for the first sale of the day, 9.30, whatever time you start, right, just before you start all feeling strong, that has no indication of where you'll be in 15 minutes. You need to stand vigil. You have to watch yourself. You have to consciously keep guard to make sure that at any moment during the day, your state does not dip down below, peak certainty when you begin an encounter. I don't mean while you're walking around or driving around all day. That's ridiculous. But when you're knocking on doors or picking up the phone, you got to be in that certain state. So keep your anchor with you at all times. Now in reality, you have to fire it off. Let's be practical. You have to always fire it off every single time. No, you don't. But I want you to understand that you have it there as your crutch. You might find yourself like, listen, 90% of the time, you're going to find yourself, maybe you're in the right state. You're on a roll. You're doing great. Things are going well. That's how you don't need to fire it off just because you're integrated. In other words, it's really more about when you start feeling yourself dipping down. In the beginning, I want you to use it more. As you get more accustomed to it, you'll probably use it less. But just remember this. Never forget there's a difference between showing up to a sale at a 10 in terms of your own state of certainty and a 9.5. They're not the same. You have to be able to have this. It's like a energy flow out of you that is just so compelling that it's a people with a sense of responsibility. You want to hear what you have to say. It comes out of you. It's palpable. Don't ever forget this. There's a reason why I can hold your attention and I can close higher than the high level of the internet anywhere else on the planet. It's something I do. It's an energy flow that comes out of me applied with the tax and strategy of the straight line. That's why I'm just beating this dead horse here with anchoring and using sense of making sure that you're consciously aware that intraday, you can't take your state as a given. You just can't. State management is the gift that you give yourself because you could have everything else right. You get it? You really worked so hard to perfect all these strategies, been through the whole training, but you forgot this one linchpin that without you showing up in a state of certainty, you got nothing to transfer. That's the bottom line. So please, if you haven't watched the live footage and been through it, take yourself through this. Get yourself into a quiet space, activate your body. Go through the process, set your anchor, and at least in the beginning, while you're conditioning it, use it again and again after a while. You're feeling great, no reason to keep using it, but always have it handy for when you dip down and be conscious. It's almost like you want to be outside your own body, constantly standing watching. Am I being my best self right now? Seriously. As a salesperson, that's what's required if you want to be one of the greats, or even average by the way. You could be an average closer, I mean your skills. Let's say you go through the straight line, and you engage, but you don't really take yourself to the highest level of proficiency. Some people are going to do that, no doubt. But you've learned how to manage your state. Guess what? You're going to do great. That good enough factor will protect you. And if you ramp it up here and you study every skills that you perfect the straight line, but ignore managing your state, guess what? The good enough factor will protect you there too. You'll have some days you'll be in the right state, so you still have stuff to transfer not as much, but you'll be okay, good enough. But when you get congruent, when you combine these two together, inner and outer, state management and transfer uncertainty, the good enough factor is a vision in the rearview mirror. You're a top producer in your field, and you're kicking butt, making a ton of money, and helping everybody you love and care about. You being a light to your community. Wow, just watch what happens, alright? So please go through the rest of this module, set your anchor, bottom line. I love you all. We'll talk again tomorrow.
